reasoning: Initial observation shows V = 20.0 with I = 2.0 and R = 10.0. To determine the relationship between V and the other variables, I should first try increasing I to see if V changes.

Increasing I from 2.0 to 3.0 resulted in V increasing from 20.0 to 30.0 while R remained constant at 10.0. This suggests V is directly proportional to I. Next, I will decrease I to observe the effect.
Decreasing I from 3.0 to 2.0 caused V to return to its original value of 20.0, confirming the direct proportionality between V and I. Now I will increase R to see if it affects V.

Increasing R from 10.0 to 20.0 while I remained constant at 2.0 caused V to increase from 20.0 to 40.0. This indicates that V is also directly proportional to R. Given the relationships observed, V appears to be the product of I and R.
